abstract: Peptide-conjugated cis / trans isomers were designed. cis -RTP interacts more stably with cell membranes than trans -RTP, and it has shown more excellent properties in inhibiting cell migration and killing cells.

title: Selectivity in the chiral self-assembly of nucleobase-arylazopyrazole photoswitches along DNA templates
abstract: The photoswitching behaviour of arylazopyrazole derivatives modified with nucleobases controls their self-assembly process along oligomeric DNA templates, opening the way to potential applications as nano- and biomaterials.

title: A biomimetic camouflaged metal organic framework for enhanced siRNA delivery in the tumor environment
abstract: An illustrative depiction highlighting the targeted drug delivery mechanism by bio-functionalized camouflaged MOF nanocarriers that enhances precision by targeting diseased cells and minimizing side effects.

title: Nanoparticles constructed from natural polyphenols are used in acute kidney injury
abstract: Acute kidney injury (AKI) is a severe clinical syndrome characterized by rapid deterioration of renal function caused by a variety of pathogeneses.

title: The heterogeneity of physiological activity for chiral carbon dots derived from l / d / dl -arginine
abstract: Chiral carbon dots synthesized from different chiral arginines have the abilities of NO production and lipid droplet inhibition, which may contribute to the therapy of cancer.

title: Radiolabeled multi-layered coated gold nanoparticles as potential biocompatible PET/SPECT tracers
abstract: The demand for tailored, disease-adapted, and easily accessible radiopharmaceuticals is one of the most persistent challenges in nuclear imaging precision medicine. In this study, two radiotracers were developed to bind SPECT and PET radionuclides.

title: Light-degradable nanocomposite hydrogels for antibacterial wound dressing applications
abstract: We developed a light-degradable nanocomposite hydrogel to achieve both controlled antibiotic delivery and hydrogel degradation using light as the sole stimulus. They could serve as novel materials for antibacterial wound dressing applications.

title: Development of silibinin-loaded nanostructured lipid carriers for Alzheimer's disease induced by amyloid beta in Wistar rats
abstract: Objective: The purpose of this study is to develop, optimize, and evaluate the in vivo effectiveness of orally administered silibinin-loaded nanostructured lipid carriers (SB-NLCs) in amyloid β-induced Alzheimer's disease in Wistar rats.

title: A laser-induced zinc oxide/graphene photoelectrode for a photocurrent-polarity-switching photoelectrochemical biosensor with bipedal DNA walker amplification
abstract: A photocurrent-polarity-switching photoelectrochemical (PEC) biosensor was developed for the ultrasensitive detection of tobramycin (TOB) through bipedal DNA walker amplification using a laser-induced zinc oxide/graphene (ZnO/LIG) photoelectrode.

title: Recent advances in chemotherapy for cancer therapy over Cu-based nanocatalysts
abstract: The recent progress of various Cu-based nanocatalysts in cancer therapy was reviewed, especially the remarkable achievements in catalytic treatment of cancer in the tumor microenvironment through CDT and CDT-involved multimodal synergies.

title: Application of a calcium and phosphorus biomineralization strategy in tooth repair: a systematic review
abstract: This review summarizes the mechanisms and commonalities of biomineralization and discusses the advancements in dental biomineralization research, guided by insights into calcium and phosphorus mineral biomineralization.

title: Nanobiopolymers in cancer therapeutics: advancing targeted drug delivery through sustainable and controlled release mechanisms
abstract: Nanobiopolymers in cancer therapeutics offer sustainable and targeted drug delivery with controlled release, enhancing treatment efficacy while ensuring sustainability and biodegradability for improved patient care.

title: Solvent-free synthesis of biostable segmented polyurethane shape memory polymers for biomedical applications
abstract: A simple, 1-pot synthesis approach is presented that produces segmented thermoplastic polyurethanes with increased molecular weight and crystallinity, shape memory properties, and the ability to form fibrous scaffolds with high strength.

title: Advances in biomimetic nanomaterial delivery systems: harnessing nature's inspiration for targeted drug delivery
abstract: The properties of nanomaterials make them promising and advantageous for use in drug delivery systems, but challenges arise from the immune system's recognition of exogenous nanoparticles, leading to their clearance and reduced targeting efficiency.

title: A Ru 3+ -functionalized-NMOF nanozyme as an inhibitor and disaggregator of β-amyloid aggregates
abstract: A novel nanozyme based on Ru 3+ -chelated nanoscale metal organic frameworks (Ru 3+ -NMOFs) displayed peroxidase-like activity, and employed in inhibiting and disaggregating β-amyloid aggregates both in vitro and in vivo .

title: Natural gambogic acid-tuned self-assembly of nanodrugs towards synergistic chemophototherapy against breast cancer
abstract: Self-assembled gambogic acid as a single-component active carrier for highly efficient synergistic and safe drug delivery against breast cancer.

title: Expression of concern: Surface modification engineering of two-dimensional titanium carbide for efficient synergistic multitherapy of breast cancer
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Surface modification engineering of two-dimensional titanium carbide for efficient synergistic multitherapy of breast cancer’ by Lei Bai et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2020, 8 , 6402–6417, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0TB01084G.

title: The effect of charge and albumin on cellular uptake of supramolecular polymer nanostructures
abstract: Bovine serum albumin (BSA) regulates cellular internalization of charged ureido-pyrimidinone (UPy)-assembly. The BSA-bond UPy-assembly can be internalized and co-localizes with mitochondria. Without BSA, the UPy-assembly anchors to the cell membrane.

title: Osteogenic differentiation of bone mesenchymal stem cells on linearly aligned triangular micropatterns
abstract: The engineered surfaces, linearly aligned triangular micropatterns (TPs) with small interspaces, promote osteogenic differentiation in BMSCs via Piezo1-mediated myosin remodeling and YAP1 nuclear translocation. Cells were enlarged and elongated.

title: Portable smartphone-enabled dydrogesterone sensors based on biomimetic polymers for personalized gynecological care
abstract: This study introduces a novel portable electrochemical sensor integrated with smartphone technology, using biomimetic polymers to accurately and affordably detect dydrogesterone in human plasma for personalized gynecological care.

title: Design of manganese-based nanomaterials for pharmaceutical and biomedical applications
abstract: Manganese-based nanostructures have significant potential as efficient diagnostic imaging contrast agents and as carrier for delivery of the drugs.

title: A broad-spectrum antibacterial hydrogel based on the synergistic action of Fmoc–phenylalanine and Fmoc–lysine in a co-assembled state
abstract: The emergence of a broad-spectrum antibacterial hydrogel by the co-assembly of minimalistic amino acid-based building blocks affords a novel class of biomaterials.

title: Tumor-targeting polymer nanohybrids with amplified ROS generation for combined photodynamic and chemodynamic therapy
abstract: Tumor-targeting polymer nanohybrids with amplified reactive oxygen species generation are developed for photodynamic and chemodynamic combinational therapy of cancer, achieving obvious inhibition of tumor growth and metastasis.

title: Bone marrow-inspired hydrogel/graphene composite scaffolds to support in vitro expansion of hematopoietic stem cells
abstract: A hydrogel/graphene composite scaffold was engineered to replicate the “soft” vascular niche and “stiff” endosteal niche within the bone marrow.

title: Development of a dextrin–vitamin D3 micelle nanocarrier for the antimicrobial peptide LLKKK18 as a potential therapeutic agent for bone infections
abstract: In this work, an expedite synthesis was developed for a self-assembled micelle carrier for the antimicrobial peptide LL18.

title: Administration sequence- and formation-dependent vaccination using acid-degradable polymeric nanoparticles with high antigen encapsulation capability
abstract: Acid-degradable polymeric nanoparticles with a high capability of GFP encapsulation demonstrate efficient antibody production in combination with booster injection of free antigens.

title: Enzyme-triggered on-demand release of a H 2 O 2 -self-supplying CuO 2 @Fe 3 O 4 nanoagent for enhanced chemodyamic antimicrobial therapy and wound healing
abstract: Schematic diagram of the preparation of the enzyme-responsive nanoplatform HA-CP@Fe 3 O 4 and its application in the study of antibacterial infection in vivo .

title: Liquid crystal polymer actuators with complex and multiple actuations
abstract: Liquid crystal polymer (LCP) actuators with complex and multiple actuations becomes a hot topic recently. Here, we summarize efforts made for achieving the goals, and furthermore, propose an outlook for LCP actuators with higher intelligence.

title: Nanomaterials and methods for cancer therapy: 2D materials, biomolecules, and molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: Nanomaterials made from 2D semiconductors and biomolecules are reviewed. Cell-nanoparticle interactions are examined in recent simulations. These nanomaterials and MD simulations reveal novel mechanisms and enable cancer drug delivery and targeting.

title: Vaporization phosphorization-mediated synthesis of phosphorus-doped TiO 2 nanocomposites for combined photodynamic and photothermal therapy of renal cell carcinoma
abstract: Under NIR II radiation excitation, P/TiO 2 nanoparticles exhibit dual functionality – effectively eradicating deep-seated tumors through the synergistic action of PDT/PTT, while affecting intra-tumor neovascularization.

title: Enzyme-responsive nanoparticles: enhancing the ability of endolysins to eradicate Staphylococcus aureus biofilm
abstract: The efficacy of peptidoglycan hydrolases (PGHs) to target staphylococcal biofilm is dramatically improved by forming enzyme-responsive polyphosphate nanoparticles (PGH–PP NPs).

title: Silver oxide decorated urchin-like microporous organic polymer composites as versatile antibacterial organic coating materials
abstract: Microporous organic polymers (MOPs) and silver oxide (Ag 2 O) hybrid composites do show good antimicrobial capabilities, making them flexible for a variety of applications.

title: The cyano positional isomerism strategy for constructing mitochondria-targeted AIEgens with type I reactive oxygen species generation capability
abstract: Based on the cyano positional isomerism strategy, a cationic AIEgen was developed, which can specifically stain mitochondria, and ablate cancer cells through potent type I photodynamic therapy both in vitro and in multicellular tumor spheroid models.

title: Design-encoded dual shape-morphing and shape-memory in 4D printed polymer parts toward cellularized vascular grafts
abstract: A combinatorial biofabrication strategy comprising design-guided shape change of 3D printed structures followed by shape recovery is demonstrated for realizing complex structures in the presence of cells, such as cellularized vascular grafts.

title: Efficient in vitro and in vivo transfection of self-amplifying mRNA with linear poly(propylenimine) and poly(ethylenimine-propylenimine) random copolymers as non-viral carriers
abstract: SaRNA was formulated with six different polymer carriers to generate saRNA-polyplexes. After screening for transfection efficiency, compared to a lipid-based standard, the best performing ones were characterized in detail and administered to mice.

title: Thermosensitive methyl-cellulose-based injectable hydrogel carrying oxaliplatin for the treatment of peritoneal metastasis in colorectal cancer
abstract: OXA-MC hydrogel was successfully synthesized and exhibited efficacy in the treatment of peritoneal metastasis in colorectal cancer.

title: Preparation and characterization of macrophage membrane camouflaged cubosomes as a stabilized and immune evasive biomimetic nano-DDS
abstract: This study aims to develop a biomimetic nano-drug delivery system (nano-DDS) by employing a macrophage cell membrane camouflaging strategy to modify lyotropic liquid crystal nanoparticles (LLC-NPs).

title: Immobilization of carbonic anhydrase on modified PES membranes for artificial lungs
abstract: We graft carbonic anhydrase (CA) efficiently onto modified polyethersulfone (PES) membranes with good blood compatibility and maintain the catalytic activity of CA for HCO 3 − to CO 2 conversion, which is exactly what the membrane of artificial lungs need.

title: Vancomycin-loaded hydrogels with thermal-responsive, self-peeling, and sustainable antibacterial properties for wound dressing
abstract: A novel hydrogel dressing with thermal-responsive self-peeling, sustained drug release, antibacterial activity, and simultaneously balanced biocompatibility is prepared by introducing poly(NIPAAm- co -NMA) and GNs into the hydrogel.

title: Flexible nanohybrid substrates utilizing gold nanocubes/nano mica platelets with 3D lightning-rod effect for highly efficient bacterial biosensors based on surface-enhanced Raman scattering
abstract: Rapid synthesis of gold nanocubes (AuNCs)/two-dimensional delaminated nano mica platelets (NMPs), controlling surface hydrophilic–hydrophobic properties for the SERS detection of different bacteria.

title: An introduction to injectable hydrogels
abstract: Julieta Paez and Khoon Lim introduce the Journal of Materials Chemistry B and Biomaterials Science themed collection on injectable hydrogels.

title: Facile one-pot synthesis of flower-like ellagic acid microparticles incorporating anti-microbial peptides for enhanced wound healing
abstract: Flower-like microparticles, AMPs@EAMPs, effectively combine antimicrobial peptides with ellagic acid, offering long-term antibacterial activity, biocompatibility, and potential for tissue repair and immune barrier reconstruction.

title: The application of nanoparticles based on ferroptosis in cancer therapy
abstract: The application of nanoparticles based on ferroptosis in cancer therapy.

abstract: Synthesis of an l -cys_rGO hydrogel and fabrication of a BSA/anti-TNF-α/ l -cys_rGO hydrogel/gSPE immunosensor.

title: Functional hemostatic hydrogels: design based on procoagulant principles

title: Microenvironment-responsive release of Mg 2+ from tannic acid decorated and multilevel crosslinked hydrogels accelerates infected wound healing
abstract: Microenvironment-responsive release of Mg 2+ from tannic acid-decorated, multilevel crosslinked hydrogels with ROS scavenging, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and tissue regenerative properties for accelerated healing of infected wounds.

title: An enhanced non-enzymatic electrochemical sensor based on the Bi 2 S 3 –TiO 2 nanocomposite with HNTs for the individual and simultaneous detection of 4-nitrophenol and nitrofurantoin in environmental samples
abstract: Graphical representation showing detecion of NFT and 4-NP in real samples.

title: A chondroitin sulphate hydrogel with sustained release of SDF-1α for extensive cartilage defect repair through induction of cell homing and promotion of chondrogenesis
abstract: Extensive cartilage damage is a significant challenge in orthopedics. This study introduces a novel P-SCMA hydrogel that promotes cell adhesion, proliferation, homing, and chondrogenesis, offering a promising solution for effective cartilage repair.

title: Engineering disease analyte response in peptide self-assembly
abstract: The need to enhance the precision and specificity of therapeutic nanocarriers has led to the development of nanoscale peptide assemblies capable of sensing and responding to disease-related analytes.

title: Nano-enzyme hydrogels for cartilage repair effectiveness based on ternary strategy therapy
abstract: An artificial nano-enzyme-enhanced hydrogel was developed to treat OA through a ternary synergistic strategy of efficiently driving O 2 production from endogenous ROS in chondrocytes and maintaining lubrication at the articular cartilage interface.

title: A separation-free paper-based hydrogel device for one-step reactive oxygen species determination by a smartphone
abstract: A hydrogel-coupled and paper-based analytical device for H 2 O 2 colorimetric detection by a smartphone was reported with a separation-free nature in complex biological samples.

abstract: Most acute cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases are caused by atherosclerotic plaque rupture leading to blocked arteries.

title: Delivery of Cu( ii ) and Mn( ii ) by polydopamine-modified nanoparticles for combined photothermal and chemotherapy
abstract: Illustration of Cu( ii ) and Mn( ii )-based nano-enzyme of chemodynamic and photothermal therapy in cells.

abstract: Liquid-phase exfoliation using biomolecules in aqueous solution is a promising approach to obtain high quality 2D nanosheets.

abstract: Design factors of PEGylated polyion complex (PIC) nanoparticles, particularly PIC vesicles, were examined regarding polyion chain matching, domain crosslinking, and chemical modification of charged units, to modulate mammalian cell-PIC interaction.

abstract: BCP ceramics regulate PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling for cell proliferation and osteogenic gene expression of BMSCs in vitro and new ectopic bone formation in the implanted ceramics in mice.

title: Toward morphologically relevant extracellular matrix: nanofiber-hydrogel composites for tumor cell culture
abstract: The natural extracellular matrix (ECM) consists of a continuous integrated fibrin network and a negatively charged proteoglycan-based matrix.

title: Surface modification of medical grade biomaterials by using a low-temperature-processed dual functional Ag–TiO 2 coating for preventing biofilm formation
abstract: Ag–TiO 2 coating on various medical-grade biomaterials demonstrates their antibacterial and antibiofilm properties.

title: A robust and biodegradable hydroxyapatite/poly(lactide- co -ε-caprolactone) electrospun membrane for dura repair
abstract: The robust and biodegradable hydroxyapatite/poly(lactide- co -ε-caprolactone) ( n HA/PLCL) electrospun membrane demonstrated anti-adhesion, prevention of CSF leakage, and pro-dura reconstruction capacities.

title: Chitosan-functionalized nanobubbles for precision oncology: advances in targeted cancer therapeutics
abstract: The convergence of nanotechnology and cancer therapeutics has opened new frontiers in the development of advanced drug delivery systems.

title: Regulation of the upconversion effect to promote the removal of biofilms on a titanium surface via photoelectrons
abstract: An upconversion effect is employed to fabricate an antibacterial surface that produces high electron density under NIR irradiation, combined with local temperature and reactive oxygen species, can eliminate the biofilm on the surface of titanium.

title: Recent advances on nanomaterial-based glutathione sensors

title: A promising ‘single’ and ‘dual’ drug-nanocomposite enriched contact lens for the management of glaucoma in response to the tear fluid enzyme
abstract: Latanoprost and latanoprost-timolol loaded contact lens for the management of glaucoma.

title: Self-healing, antibiofouling and anticorrosion properties enabled by designing polymers with dynamic covalent bonds and responsive linkages
abstract: The combination of self-healing property and controlled release of corrosion inhibitor yield polymer coatings with excellent anticorrosion properties.

title: β-Glycosidase sensitive oral nanoparticles for combined photothermal and chemo treatment of colorectal cancer
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the most common malignant tumors in the world, and its treatment strategies mainly include surgical resection, chemotherapy, adjuvant radiotherapy, and immunotherapy.

title: Near-infrared-driven upconversion nanoparticles with photocatalysts through water-splitting towards cancer treatment
abstract: NIR-driven UCNPs with photocatalysts can absorb UV/vis light via water splitting. Water splitting enables in situ H 2 , O 2 , and OH˙ generation under a light source. The nanocomposites can enhance therapeutic approaches for cancer treatment.

title: Fabrication and characterization of calcium peroxide and berberine loaded cryogels for enhanced wound healing
abstract: This study explores cryogels, loaded with CP and berberine BB for wound healing. The cryogels exhibited high porosity, biocompatibility, and sustained release of CP and BB. The scaffolds accelerated wound closure and improved healing in a rat model.

title: Supramolecular assembly of isomeric SN-38 prodrugs regulated by conjugation sites
abstract: By attaching hydrophilic OEG on different conjugation sites of SN38, isomeric self-assembling prodrugs were developed and self-assembled into giant nanotubes and filamentous assemblies, revealing the critical contribution of conjugation sites.

title: Silk–gelatin hybrid hydrogel: a potential carrier for RNA therapeutics
abstract: A silk fibroin (SF) and gelatin (Gel) hydrogel has been developed to stabilize RNA and enable regulated release. As per findings, SF interacts more with RNA than collagen, gel, and chitosan. This provides valuable perspectives on developing RNA therapies for use in biological applications.

title: Cobalt-based hybrid nanoparticles loaded with curcumin for ligand-enhanced synergistic nanocatalytic therapy/chemotherapy combined with calcium overload
abstract: CaCO 3 -mineralized cobalt-based nanocatalysts were synthesized and loaded with curcumin for synergistic nanocatalytic therapy/chemotherapy combined with calcium overload. Curcumin acts as a ligand and an electron donor to enhance the efficacy of nanocatalytic therapy.

title: A liquid metal/polypyrrole electrospun TPU composite conductive network for highly sensitive strain sensing in human motion monitoring
abstract: Developing soft wearable sensors with high sensitivity, low cost, and a wide monitoring range is crucial for monitoring human health.

title: Acellular dermal matrix hydrogels promote healing of radiation-induced skin injury in a rat model
abstract: Using a novel rat model of RISI, the research demonstrates that ADM hydrogel effectively reduces wound area and severity, increases epithelial thickness, and accelerates healing compared to controls.

title: Heparin binding induced supramolecular chirality into the self-assembly of perylenediimide bolaamphiphile
abstract: Heparin, an anionic chiral biomolecule, binds to cationic achiral perylenediimide derivative to induce supramolecular helicity, forming chiral nanostructures in both aqueous and cell culture medium.

title: Trimetallic nanocomposites developed for efficient in vivo bimodal imaging via fluorescence and magnetic resonance
abstract: Development of a functional protein-templated nanocomposite containing luminescent Au–Ag nanoclusters and SPION, successfully applied as a biocompatible contrast agent in living mice.

title: Bioactive hydrogels based on lysine dendrigrafts as crosslinkers: tailoring elastic properties to influence hMSC osteogenic differentiation
abstract: Optimizing hydrogels for tissue engineering with multivalent crosslinking lysine dendrigrafts.

title: Advanced nanomaterials for electrochemical sensors: application in wearable tear glucose sensing technology
abstract: The recent applications of nanomaterials in tear fluid electrochemical bio-glucose sensors are systematically reviewed. The challenges, strategies, and prospects for those bio-glucose sensor nanomaterials are also emphasized.

title: Immunomodulatory hydrogels for skin wound healing: cellular targets and design strategy
abstract: Schematic illustration of roles and targets of immunoregulatory cells and hydrogel design strategies to promote wound healing.

title: Colloidal photonic crystals towards biological applications
abstract: Colloidal photonic crystals exhibit unique properties that make them ideal for biological applications. This perspective summarizes the primary properties, the strategies, and recent advancements for biological applications.

title: Advances in osteoimmunomodulation of biomaterials after intrabone implantation: focus on surface hydrophilicity
abstract: Hydrophilicity of implantable biomaterials can facilitate osteoimmunomodulation through influencing proteins adsorption, blood clotting, neutrophils activation, macrophages polarization, angiogenesis and osteogenesis to enhance osseointegration.

title: Sculpturing the future of water-soluble cyclodextrin branched polymers in pharmaceutical applications
abstract: Versatile water-soluble cyclodextrin polymers can be easily prepared from naturally occurring monomers upon polycondensation in the presence of crosslinking agents. We consider their potential use in combination therapies and the hurdles faced.

title: A zirconia/tantalum biocermet: in vitro and in vivo evaluation for biomedical implant applications
abstract: New zirconia/20 vol% tantalum (3Y-TZP/Ta) biocermets exhibit exceptional capabilities due to a combination of properties that make them highly suitable for biomedical applications in orthopedics, dentistry and other areas of hard tissue replacement.

title: Correction: Investigating the effect of acidic and basic precipitation on the antibacterial activity of ZnO nanoparticles against Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacteria
abstract: Correction for ‘Investigating the effect of acidic and basic precipitation on the antibacterial activity of ZnO nanoparticles against Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacteria’ by Sreekanth R. et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2024, 12 , 2180–2196, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TB02119J.

abstract: A triphasic GELMA based scaffold was used for osteochondral repair. The upper layer was bonded with chondroitin sulfate, the middle and lower layers introduced hydroxyapatite, promoting chondrogenic, angiogenic and osteogenic induction capabilities.

title: Progress in ultrasmall ferrite nanoparticles enhanced T 1 magnetic resonance angiography
abstract: Schematic diagram of design, regulation, and application of UFNPs.

title: Design and development of 3D printed shape memory triphasic polymer-ceramic bioactive scaffolds for bone tissue engineering
abstract: Scaffolds for bone tissue engineering require considerable mechanical strength to repair damaged bone defects.

abstract: The integration capitalizes on the free radical scavenging abilities of cerium-based materials, boosting the system's antioxidant capacity. This novel drug delivery preserves the tumor suppressive impact of the anticancer drugs.

title: Bioinspired colloidal crystal hydrogel pressure sensors with Janus wettability for uterus cervical canal tension perception
abstract: A cervical pressure sensor with stress sensing and selective adhesion inspired by the Janus wettability of lotus leaves.

title: Dual-mode fluorescence and electrochemiluminescence sensors based on Ru-MOF nanosheets for sensitive detection of apoE genes
abstract: A novel FL–ECL dual-mode sensor for apoE gene detection with ruthenium MOF nanosheets exhibits high sensitivity, wide linear ranges, and robust real-world application potential.

title: Transparent silk fibroin film-facilitated infected-wound healing through antibacterial, improved fibroblast adhesion and immune modulation
abstract: Pluronic F127 modified silk fibroin film could load different types of antibacterial agents to accelerate wound recovery by promoting fibroblast adhesion, eradicating bacteria and facilitating angiogenesis and re-epithelialization.

title: pH-Sensitive doxorubicin delivery using zinc oxide nanoparticles as a rectified theranostic platform: in vitro anti-proliferative, apoptotic, cell cycle arrest and in vivo radio-distribution studies
abstract: An innovative pH-responsive and biocompatible agent (Tc-99m labelled DOX-loaded ZnO@dextran) is developed as an intelligent platform for tumor-targeted theranostic applications.

title: Investigating the self-assembly of 2NapFF and ureido-pyrimidinone multicomponent systems for cell culture
abstract: Low molecular weight gelators have mechanical properties ideal for regenerative medicine. This study explores the combination of a known UPy-system with a dipeptide gelator, 2NapFF, examining each system across multiple length scales.

title: Inherently targeted estradiol-derived carbon dots for selective killing of ER (+) breast cancer cells via oridonin-triggered p53 pathway activation
abstract: E2-CA-CD, a target specific estradiol-derived carbon dot, diagnosed ER (+) MCF-7 cells via bioimaging, distinguishing them from ER (−) MDA-MB-231 and NIH3T3 cells. It delivered oridonin to MCF-7 cells, inducing apoptosis by p53 pathway upregulation.

title: Simulation of the bone remodelling microenvironment by calcium compound-loaded hydrogel fibrous membranes for in situ bone regeneration
abstract: The calcium compound-loaded hydrogel fibrous membranes are prepared as the guided bone regeneration membranes to activate the endogenous regenerative capability of bone to regenerate the calvarial critical-sized defects.

title: Harnessing the power of gold: advancements in anticancer gold complexes and their functionalized nanoparticles
abstract: Captivating anticancer properties of gold complexes and functionalized gold nanoparticles.

title: Injectable and self-healable nano-architectured hydrogel for NIR-light responsive chemo- and photothermal bacterial eradication
abstract: A stimuli-responsive, injectable, and in situ -forming hydrogel with synergistic, self-healing, and on-demand drug-delivery and antibacterial properties.

title: Hydrogels: a promising therapeutic platform for inflammatory skin diseases treatment
abstract: Hydrogels revolutionize treating chronic skin diseases by enhancing drug delivery and reducing side effects. This review explores hydrogel design, drug integration, and clinical trials, highlighting their potential to improve efficacy and safety.

title: A whole-course-repair system based on ROS/glucose stimuli-responsive EGCG release and tunable mechanical property for efficient treatment of chronic periodontitis in diabetic rats
abstract: A whole-course-repair system based on ROS/glucose dual responsive, injectable and multifunctional hydrogel to release EGCG is reported for the treatment of CPDM.

title: A robust and versatile host–guest peptide toolbox for developing highly stable and specific quantum dot-based peptide probes for imaging extracellular matrices and cells
abstract: We have developed a robust and versatile host–guest peptide toolbox to construct highly stable and specific HGP-QDs probes for imaging extracellular matrices and cells, providing an efficient tool for multiplex imaging and precision medicine.

title: Reconfiguring the endogenous electric field of a wound through a conductive hydrogel for effective exudate management to enhance skin wound healing
abstract: In this work, we proposed a mechanism for hydrogel-based dressings to respond to endogenous electric fields through exudate absorption and conducted a verification study using polyacrylamide and sodium alginate (PAM–SA) hydrogels.

title: Enhancing cell adhesion in synthetic hydrogels via physical confinement of peptide-functionalized polymer clusters

title: Fructose-mineralized black phosphorus for syncretic bone regeneration and tumor suppression

title: Development of quaternary ammonium based acrylic copolymer antimicrobial coatings for polyurethane tracheoesophageal voice prostheses

title: Tetramethylpyrazine-loaded electroconductive hydrogels promote tissue repair after spinal cord injury by protecting the blood–spinal cord barrier and neurons

title: Biodegradable MoN x @Mo-foil electrodes for human-friendly supercapacitors

title: pH-responsive iron-loaded carbonaceous nanoparticles for chemodynamic therapy based on the Fenton reaction

title: Influence of lipid vesicle properties on the function of conjugation dependent membrane active peptides
abstract: Membrane active peptides (MAPs) can provide novel means to trigger the release of liposome encapsulated drugs to improve the efficacy of liposomal drug delivery systems.

title: New insights into the photophysical properties and interaction mechanisms of Janus green blue dye with polyanions and its applications in colorimetric visualization of loop-mediated isothermal amplification and polymerase chain reaction
abstract: Photophysical properties and binding mechanisms of Janus green blue (JGB) with polyanions were studied. DNA induced JGB aggregation was utilized to develop sensitive colorimetric assays for LAMP and PCR, detecting S. pneumoniae and MTF-1 genes.

title: Nanoparticles targeting the adenosine pathway for cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Outline of this review on adenosine pathway inhibitors for cancer immunotherapy.

title: Development and characterization of a novel poly( N -isopropylacrylamide)-based thermoresponsive photoink and its applications in DLP bioprinting
abstract: Biomedical applications of the 3D-printed thermoresponsive hydrogels developed using our DLP-compatible versatile photoink.

title: Photocaging of amino acids and short peptides by arylidenethiazoles: mechanism, photochemical characteristics and biological behaviour
abstract: Novel biocompatible photocleavable protective groups for photocaging of amino acids and peptides have been synthesised. The mechanism of effective release of biomolecules has been studied in detail.

title: Chemo-photothermal therapy of bacterial infections using metal–organic framework-integrated polymeric network coatings

title: Advances in reparative materials for infectious bone defects and their applications in maxillofacial regions

abstract: Thiol-containing hyperbranched polysiloxane, a unconventional luminescent polymer, for scavenging intracellular reactive oxygen species.

title: Bio-conjugated carbon dots for the bimodal detection of prostate cancer biomarkers via sandwich fluorescence and electrochemical immunoassays
abstract: A bio-conjugated carbon dot-based detection probe enabled bimodal (fluorescence and electrochemical) detection strategy for the determination of prostate specific antigen in a blood serum sample.

title: Electrospun organic/inorganic hybrid nanofibers for accelerating wound healing: a review
abstract: This review summarizes fabrication strategies and manifold applications of electrospun organic/inorganic hybrid nanofibers in promoting the wound healing process.

title: Nanoassemblies with Gd-chelating lipids (GMO@DTPA-BSA-Gd) as a potential new type of high molecular weight contrast agents
abstract: Self-assembled lipid nanoparticles containing Gd-chelating lipids are a new type of positive magnetic resonance imaging contrast agents.

title: A disulfide molecule–vancomycin nanodrug delivery system efficiently eradicates intracellular bacteria
abstract: A thiol-mediated nanodrug delivery system was developed to improve the cell membrane penetration efficiency and intracellular antibacterial activities of antibiotics.

title: Porous helical supramolecular columns self-organized via the fluorophobic effect of a semifluorinated tapered dendron
abstract: Fluorophobic effect enhances supramolecular microsegregation.

title: A natural multifunction and multiscale hierarchical matrix as a drug-eluting scaffold for biomedical applications
abstract: Sea urchin spines are biogenic single crystals of magnesium calcite that are stiff, strong, damage tolerant and light and have a bicontinuous porous structure. Thanks to these peculiar characteristics they can be used as drug-releasing platforms for spine fusion.

title: Exceptional stability of ultrasmall cubic copper metal nanoclusters – a molecular dynamics study
abstract: The cubic metal nanoclusters, especially the smaller ones, exhibited stability when placed in 4 M [N1114][C1SO3] ionic liquid solution.

title: The current status and development trend of hydrogel application in spinal surgery
abstract: Spinal diseases often result in compromised mobility and diminished quality of life due to the intricate anatomy surrounding the nervous system.

title: An injectable photocuring silk fibroin-based hydrogel for constructing an antioxidant microenvironment for skin repair
abstract: An injectable photocurable hydrogel based on methacrylated dopamine and silk fibroin constructs an antioxidant protein microenvironment, which prevents oxidative stress in vitro, and promotes collagen disposition and angiogenesis in vivo .

title: Combining sandblasting and pink anodisation of Ti implants as a promising method for improving fibroblast adhesion and immune response
abstract: This study is performed with the aim of enhancing the cell response to titanium surfaces through sandblasting and pink anodizing (MC04 + PA), correlating in vitro findings with protein adsorption patterns.

title: Calcium-enriched carbon nanoparticles loaded with indocyanine green for near-infrared fluorescence imaging-guided synergistic calcium overload, photothermal therapy, and glutathione-depletion-enhanced photodynamic therapy
abstract: Calcium-enriched carbon nanoparticles loaded with indocyanine green for near-infrared fluorescence imaging-guided synergistic calcium overload, photothermal therapy, and GSH-depletion-enhanced photodynamic therapy.

title: Strategies to improve the performance of polyetheretherketone (PEEK) as orthopedic implants: from surface modification to addition of bioactive materials
abstract: Various modification strategies have been used to improve the physicochemical and biological properties of PEEK as orthopedic implants.

title: A chitosan–α-naphthaldehyde hydrogel film containing pineapple leaf fibers for wound dressing applications
abstract: A biodegradable chitosan–α-naphthaldehyde hydrogel film was synthesized, for in vitro controlled drug release studies. Infusion with pineapple leaf fibers increases its mechanical strength and may be used further for wound dressing applications.

title: Rationally designed protein A surface molecularly imprinted magnetic nanoparticles for the capture and detection of Staphylococcus aureus
abstract: Staphyloccus aureus (S. aureus) capture and detection using protein A surface imprinted magnetic nanoparticles.

title: Silica nanoparticle conjugation with gallic acid towards enhanced free radical scavenging capacity and activity on osteosarcoma cells in vitro
abstract: Dendritic mesoporous silica nanoparticles are surface-modified with gallic acid via carbamate or amide bond formation. The different GA-conjugated materials showed tuneable antioxidant activity in osteosarcoma cells and inhibited their migration.

title: Schottky heterojunction CeO 2 @MXene nanosheets with synergistic type I and type II PDT for anti-osteosarcoma
abstract: Endogenous O 2 can be continuously generated by the catalysis of Ce in the lattice of CeO 2 to enhance 1 O 2 production via a type II PDT (energy transfer), and many other ROS (such as O 2 − and ·OH) can be produced via a type I PDT (electron transfer).

title: Revealing bactericidal events on graphene oxide nano films deposited on metal implant surfaces
abstract: A graphene oxide nano film, known for its insulating properties, when deposited on conducting substrates, redirects the flow of electrons, leading to the death of bacteria.

title: A highly conductive, robust, self-healable, and thermally responsive liquid metal-based hydrogel for reversible electrical switches
abstract: A PNIPAM/LMPs hydrogel exhibits the highest electrical switching, with an unprecedented switch of 6.1 orders of magnitude. The hydrogel also possesses robust mechanical properties, a tissue-like compression modulus and self-healing capabilities.

title: Harnessing multifunctional collagen mimetic peptides to create bioinspired stimuli responsive hydrogels for controlled cell culture
abstract: Hybrid polymer–peptide hydrogels utilize fibrillary-assembled collagen mimetic peptides to create materials with physical and covalent crosslinks. These tunable hydrogels exhibit strain and temperature responsiveness and promote cell motility.

title: A multimodal therapy for infected diabetic wounds based on glucose-responsive nanocomposite-integrated microneedles
abstract: Antibacterial nanocomposite-integrated microneedles were designed for infected diabetic wound therapy. The nanocomposite could be destroyed by high glucose and exerted a powerful and synergistic antibacterial effect against bacterial infections.

title: Copper-based carbon dots modified hydrogel with osteoimmunomodulatory and osteogenesis for bone regeneration
abstract: Biomaterials with dual functions of osteoimmunomodulation and bone repair are very promising in the field of orthopedic materials.

title: A DNA nanowire based-DNAzyme walker for amplified imaging of microRNA in tumor cells
abstract: We propose a DNAzyme walker constructed from aptamer-modified DNA nanowires, which can efficiently penetrate target cells without the assistance of non-DNA substances. This strategy enables sensitive and effective imaging of miRNA in tumor cells.

title: Functionalities of pH-responsive DNA nanostructures in tumor-targeted strategies
abstract: Design and application of various pH-responsive DNA nanostructures for tumor-targeted strategies toward extracellular and intracellular environments are comprehensively reviewed. Their potential and challenges are also critically discussed.

title: In vitro and in vivo biocompatibility assessment of chalcogenide thermoelectrics as implants
abstract: This study conducted the first comprehensive in vitro and in vivo biocompatibility assessment of twelve typical sulfide compounds, identifying promising thermoelectric biomaterials and establishing a foundation for their application in biomedicine.

title: Enhancing spleen-targeted mRNA delivery with branched biodegradable tails in lipid nanoparticles
abstract: Ionizable lipids with branched tails enhance the selective mRNA delivery to spleen, particularly to antigen-presenting cells.
